subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: Suppose that the function $f(t)$ defined below is the density function of some random variable $X$. $f(t) = \begin{cases} e^{-t} & t \ge 0, \\ 0 & t < 0. \end{cases}$ Compute $\Pr(-10 \le X \le 10)$.
explanation: $\Pr(-10 \le X \le 10) = \int_{-10}^{10} f(t)\,dt = \int_{-10}^0 f(t)\,dt + \int_0^{10} f(t)\,dt = \int_0^{10} e^{-t}\,dt = -e^{-t}\Big|_0^{10} = 1 - e^{-10}$. A pdf need not be continuous, as the following example illustrates.
